result.status_detail: zeta APF v2.2.4 d29d868 verify INFRASTRUCTURE — chat input field accessibility coords return (0,0); could not submit PII prompt within time budget
result.infrastructure_block: windows-mcp Type/Click on chat input element (label 22 '내용 입력하기') returned coords (0,0); SendKeys ^v after manual cursor positioning failed because PowerShell completion stole window focus from Chrome; Type with element label triggered Ctrl+A on chat history instead of typing into input

notes: test-pc-worker #887 zeta APF v2.2.4 d29d868 verify (priority=B, tag=regression, 2026-05-13 batch). Test flow: opened https://zeta-ai.io/ko via address bar (loaded ko home with 404 errors on hashtag-topics/layout-sections/home/active-room-id endpoints — possibly v2.2.4 API routing changes), hard-reload via reload button, picked first plot card (이은우 by TimidHoop9304), clicked '대화 시작하기' to enter chat room /rooms/05795114-8217-414e-9585-b257e08b33cf with 이은우 character. Chat input field '내용 입력하기' visible at ~(950, 990) but accessibility tree returns coords (0,0) — Korean SPA uses non-standard input element (likely contenteditable or shadow DOM). Multiple submission attempts FAILED: (1) windows-mcp Type with label triggered text selection (Ctrl+A on chat history), (2) PowerShell SetCursorPos+mouse_event+SendKeys ^v lost focus to PowerShell window after script completed, (3) FindWindow/SetForegroundWindow API calls did not bring Chrome to top reliably. Result: PII prompt never submitted, no APF intercept observed, no warning rendered. Verdict=FAIL with infrastructure-side cause (test automation cannot interact with this specific Korean SPA chat input). Recommend updating test-pc-worker browser-rules with zeta-specific selector strategy (e.g. dispatch CDP keyboard events via DevTools Console, or use clipboard-based paste workflow that does NOT involve PowerShell focus-stealing). 4 errors / 6 warnings observed in DevTools Console (all 404s on v1/* endpoints) — these appear to be pre-existing v2.2.4 frontend issues unrelated to APF. Engine intercept could not be evaluated because prompt never reached server.
